author: Авакумов Данил Эдуардович
objective: Map how evaluation noise parameters determine emergent cooperative role ratios in the coevolution system.

key_findings: Empirical law w ∝ sigma^1.57 / mu^0.60 strongly describes equilibrium.
Direct weight injection does not improve performance.
Survival-pressure scaling does not improve performance.
background.hypothesis: Emergent cooperative architecture is governed by evaluation noise regime rather than role weights or explicit selection scaling.

research_hypothesis.statement: Emergent role ratios depend on the noise regime induced by evaluation sampling parameters.

experiment_blocks.title: PARAMETER GRID EXECUTION
experiment_blocks.goal: Run coevolution across full noise parameter grid.

experiment_blocks.title: ROLE-RATIO SURFACE
experiment_blocks.goal: Compute emergent role ratios across parameter space.

experiment_blocks.title: NOISE VS ARCHITECTURE ANALYSIS
experiment_blocks.goal: Measure correlation between evaluation noise and role ratios.

experiment_blocks.title: PERFORMANCE REGION IDENTIFICATION
experiment_blocks.goal: Identify regions of parameter space with highest cooperative performance.

experiment_blocks.title: ARCHITECTURE PHASE DIAGRAM
experiment_blocks.goal: Construct phase diagram linking noise regime to emergent cooperative architecture.

experiment_blocks.title: FINAL REPORT
experiment_blocks.goal: Document noise-regime influence on cooperative architecture.

definition_of_done: Role ratios computed for all settings.
